economic_notes: First Nation has internally restricted capital bylaw funds (Infrastructure, Capital, Economic Development) totaling $2,648,547 in portfolio investments. GBEs include TTQ Economic Development Corporation, Takem Ti Qwelsa7 Eco-Resource Corporation, Xa'xtsa Gas Station Ltd., 1020376 B.C. Ltd (33.33%), Tsetspa7 Forestry Limited Partnership (16.67%), and Tsetspa7 Loakam (Logging) Ltd. Harrison Hydro LP generates revenue. St'at'imc (PC) 2011 Trust settlement funds flow to community.
notes: Audit is qualified on two grounds: (1) funded tangible capital assets including roads, bridges, buildings, water and utility systems not capitalized in prior years; (2) GBE/GBP financial information not available and not accounted for in consolidated statements. Six GBEs/GBPs accounted for by modified equity method. Prior year (2020) figures restated for under-accrual of consulting fees and over-accrual of payroll remittances. Long-term debt total of $4,007,524 includes demand loans ($1,562,242), CMHC mortgages ($2,356,607), long-term debt ($99,651), and capital lease ($49,624). Annual debt service calculated as principal repayments: long-term debt $84,529 + CMHC mortgages $115,080 + demand loans $53,789 + capital lease $12,979 = $266,377. Total government transfers includes ISC $2,027,720 + Province of BC $322,867 + CMHC $134,343 + First Nations Health Authority $2,252,443 + FNESC $52,546 + Dept of Natural Resources $102,989 + Funding from First Nation organizations $91,174 = approx $4,984,082 plus deferred revenue movement; OSR approximated. No schedule of remuneration provided in the excerpted text.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
